novel_features.name: Charge & Edit Calendar
novel_features.description: Every upcoming cycle's charge date, edit-lock deadline, and delivery date in one timeline so you never miss an edit window or get surprised by a charge.
novel_features.rationale: Charge = delivery-7d and lock = delivery-5d (-3d Fresh) must be derived across many cycles by joining snapshotted delivery calendar with plan cadence; no single endpoint returns all your money/edit dates.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this before any basket edit to answer 'can I still change the order / when does money leave the account'.

novel_features.name: Basket Diff
novel_features.description: Compares your current recurring basket against a previous cycle's snapshot to show exactly what was added, dropped, or re-quantified before the template locks.
novel_features.rationale: Requires two basket states over time; the API only returns the current /cart/summary and the web UI never persists last cycle's template.
novel_features.example: shopper basket diff --from last-cycle --to current --agent --select added,removed,price_changed
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to audit what changed before confirming a cycle, or to explain why this charge differs from the last.

novel_features.name: Price Watch
novel_features.description: Tracks the price history of the SKUs you actually buy and alerts when one rises or drops meaningfully versus your own purchase baseline.
novel_features.rationale: /catalog/search returns only today's price; a personal 'more expensive than I paid' baseline needs periodic local price snapshots the API never keeps.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this before confirming a cycle to catch staples that quietly got pricier, or find real drops worth stocking up on.

novel_features.name: Restock Predictor
novel_features.description: Predicts when you'll run out of each staple from your historical buying cadence and suggests what to add to the upcoming basket.
novel_features.rationale: Needs a per-SKU purchase time series (cadence + quantity across cycles) joined against the current basket; no endpoint exposes a consumption model.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to proactively fill the next basket so the household doesn't run out of cafe/arroz/fralda mid-cycle.

novel_features.name: Catalog Drift Detector
novel_features.description: Flags products you buy that were discontinued, silently swapped, or kept their price while shrinking the pack, surfacing the real R$/kg or R$/L change.
novel_features.rationale: Detecting shrinkflation or disappearance requires comparing successive catalog snapshots (price, pack size, availability) per SKU and normalizing to unit price; a single /catalog/search call has no temporal reference.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when the user asks 'why is my bill the same but I have less', or to auto-find replacements for staples that vanished.

novel_features.name: Cashback Threshold Optimizer
novel_features.description: Computes the cheapest set of items to add (or whether to wait) to cross the next cashback tier, favoring things you'll need anyway.
novel_features.rationale: Combines current cart total, the active cashback rule, and predicted restock demand into a knapsack over your own demand; no endpoint computes cheapest add-ons to reach a tier.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this near the edit deadline to decide whether topping up the basket earns net-positive cashback without buying junk.

narrative.headline: The first CLI for Shopper — every catalog, cart, and delivery surface plus a local price/basket history the web app throws away each cycle.
narrative.value_prop: Shopper's recurring basket, fixed charge-7-days-before clock, and drifting prices produce a time series the official app discards every cycle. This CLI keeps it in a local SQLite store, unlocking charge-calendar, basket diff, price-watch, restock prediction, catalog-drift detection, and cashback optimization — none of which any Shopper interface offers.
narrative.auth_narrative: Shopper uses a Bearer JWT. Sign in at shopper.com.br in your browser, copy the token from the Authorization header of any siteapi.shopper.com.br request (DevTools > Network), and set SHOPPER_TOKEN. The CLI also sends app-os-x-version and your store context automatically.

quickstart.comment: Health check; verifies token + store context without a live call.
Search the catalog — the most common first action.
See your current recurring basket total and item count.
See upcoming charge dates and edit deadlines.

troubleshoots.fix: Set SHOPPER_TOKEN to a fresh Bearer JWT from a logged-in browser session (DevTools > Network > any siteapi request).
Select a store first: shopper features select <id>; the store context (x-store-id) is required for catalog and cart.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI to inspect and reason about a Shopper grocery subscription: search the catalog, read the recurring basket and delivery schedule, and answer cross-cycle questions about price changes, charge dates, restock timing, and cashback. It shines for questions the web UI can't answer because it only shows the current cycle.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI to place or pay for an order — checkout/payment is intentionally out of scope.
Do not use it for non-Shopper grocery services (iFood, Daki, Rappi, Carrefour).
Do not use it to change account passwords or payment cards.

recipes.title: Find a staple and check your price baseline
recipes.command: shopper catalog search 'cafe pilao' --agent --select results.name,results.price && shopper price-watch --only-basket --threshold 5%
recipes.explanation: Search the catalog, then see whether your tracked staples drifted in price.

recipes.title: Pre-deadline cycle review
recipes.command: shopper charge-calendar --locking-soon --agent && shopper basket diff --from last-cycle --to current --agent
recipes.explanation: Check which cycle locks soon and audit what changed before it does.

recipes.title: Hit the cashback tier efficiently
recipes.explanation: Decide the cheapest useful top-up to earn cashback.

trigger_phrases: did my shopper prices go up
what should I restock on shopper
